releases.author: iY0Yi
releases.description:
- Add click-to-edit for GLSL code blocks in Live Preview (both with and without @viewer)
- Fix copy button: use Obsidian native setIcon instead of broken custom SVG
- Add copy button to @viewer blocks (edit mode and reading mode)
- Make copy button hover-only to match native Obsidian behavior
- Extract shared addCopyButton/navigateToCodeBlock helpers
- Hide native edit-block-button in Live Preview (replaced by click-to-edit)

* Use sentence case for settings labels by @iY0Yi in https://github.com/iY0Yi/obsidian_glslviewer/pull/1
* Remove obsolete community plugin listing by @iY0Yi in https://github.com/iY0Yi/obsidian_glslviewer/pull/2

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/iY0Yi/obsidian_glslviewer/compare/0.1.4...0.1.5

releases.author: iY0Yi
releases.description:
# 🎨 v0.1.1 - CSS Snippets Support & Code Block Improvements

## ✨ What's New

### 🔧 Full CSS Snippets Compatibility
- GLSL code blocks without `@viewer` directive now properly support CSS snippets
- Custom themes and code block styling work as expected
- No more style conflicts with community themes

### 🌈 Stable Syntax Highlighting
- Fixed inconsistent syntax highlighting for non-viewer GLSL blocks
- Reliable highlighting on note switching and initial page loads
- Beautiful code display every time

### 📝 Better Standard Code Block Support
- Standard Obsidian features (copy button, etc.) work perfectly
- Significantly reduced conflicts with other plugins
- Seamless integration with existing workflows

### 🔄 Clear Usage Patterns
````glsl
```glsl
// @viewer
// ↑ GLSL Viewer with WebGL rendering
void mainImage(out vec4 fragColor, in vec2 fragCoord) {
    // Interactive shader + code display
}
```
````

````glsl
```glsl
// ↑ Standard GLSL code block (no @viewer)
// CSS snippets, syntax highlighting, copy button included
void someFunction() {
    // Code display only
}
```
````

### 🎯 Perfect for
- CSS Snippets enthusiasts: Style your GLSL blocks exactly how you want
- Theme creators: Consistent code block design across all blocks
- Multi-plugin users: Better compatibility with Shiki Highlighter and others

### 🔧 Compatibility
- Works with: Shiki Highlighter, Code Styler, custom themes
- Obsidian: v1.0.0+
- No breaking changes: All existing `@viewer` blocks continue to work

### 📦 Installation
- Obsidian > Settings > Community plugins
- Update GLSL Viewer plugin
- Enjoy improved CSS snippets and syntax highlighting!

---
Note: This update primarily enhances the experience for GLSL blocks without `@viewer`. All existing shader viewers remain fully functional.
releases.name: v0.1.1

releases.author: iY0Yi
releases.description:
# 🎨 GLSL Viewer Plugin 0.1.0

A GLSL preview plugin for Obsidian that enables real-time WebGL rendering with Shadertoy-style shader code.

## ✨ Features

- 📦 **Shadertoy-compatible** - Use familiar `mainImage()` function
- 🎮 **Interactive controls** - Play/pause animations
- 🖼️ **Texture support** - Load textures from your vault (iChannel0-3)
- ⚙️ **Configurable** - Aspect ratio, autoplay, and more
- 🔧 **Easy setup** - Simple code block syntax
- 🎨 Template system for complex shader patterns
- 
## 🚀 Quick Start

Create a code block with `glsl` language with `//@viewer` directive:
````glsl
```glsl
//@viewer
void mainImage(out vec4 fragColor, in vec2 fragCoord) {
    vec2 uv = fragCoord / iResolution.xy;
    vec3 col = 0.5 + 0.5 * cos(iTime + uv.xxy + vec3(0, 2, 4));
    fragColor = vec4(col, 1.0);
}
```
````
## 📋 Available Uniforms

- `vec3 iResolution` - Screen resolution
- `float iTime` - Elapsed time  
- `sampler2D iChannel0-3` - Texture inputs
- `vec4 iMouse` - Mouse position
- And more...

## 📦 Installation

1. Download the latest release files
2. Copy to your Obsidian plugins folder
3. Enable in Community plugins settings

For detailed documentation, see the [README](https://github.com/your-username/obsidian_glslviewer#readme).

---

**Requirements:** Obsidian v1.0.0+
releases.name: GLSL Viewer Plugin 0.1.0
